summary: Iteration 2 verification: full backend + frontend regression on top of new public marketing layer + 3 free AI tools + new navy/gold palette. 32/32 backend pytest cases pass (21 from iter1 + 11 new public-tool tests covering /api/public/decode-statement-text, /decode-statement file upload, /budget-calc, /price-check, IP rate limit 5/30days). Frontend marketing pages (/, /pricing, /trust, /ai-tools, /ai-tools/*) render with new palette, persona on-ramp works, embedded Statement Decoder runs end-to-end (Claude Sonnet 4.5 returns summary + line items + anomalies in <15s), countdown to 1 Jul 2026 displays correctly (58 days / 01 hrs / 08 min), Pricing tier cards show 'Most popular' gold badge, Trust + Footer expose all 4 Australian crisis numbers (1800 353 374, 1800 700 600, 1300 22 4636, 13 11 14), mobile hamburger toggles. Existing authenticated product (cathy@example.com login → dashboard with 3 streams + lifetime cap + 'Things to know' anomalies + plain-English summary, sidebar nav across statements/chat/family/audit, /login redirects to /app while authed) still fully functional under new palette. Zero JS pageerrors across both flows.

minor.issue: Static hard-coded medians for 10 services — acknowledged in PRD as PLACEHOLDER, real medians pending accumulated user data
RATE_LIMIT_BUCKET is in-memory (defaultdict) — resets on backend restart; for production should be Redis-backed, but acceptable for MVP
Static sample (Sarah, 10:00 AM) — flagged in iter1, still mocked

issues: Counters '2,847 households / 127 advisor practices / $2.4M flagged' are STATIC PLACEHOLDERS — already documented in PRD/mocked APIs list

data-testid attributes on hero embedded decoder elements (e.g. 'embedded-decoder', 'decode-btn', 'countdown') are not present — script falls back to text selectors which still works, but hardening with explicit testids would improve future test stability. Not a blocker.

action_items: Optional: add data-testid attributes to landing-hero countdown, embedded decoder, and decode button for stable selector-based UI tests.

Optional (production hardening): move RATE_LIMIT_BUCKET from in-memory dict to Redis or DB-backed counter so backend restart doesn't reset abuse-limit windows, and so multiple worker processes share the bucket.
Optional (iter1 carry-over): convert /api/auth/me missing-token from 403 → 401; replace static participant appointment with real calendar feed; expose Statement Upload in sidebar nav.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew from 545 → 738 lines with the public layer added. Strongly recommend splitting into routers (auth/household/statements/budget/chat/family/audit/participant/public) — at this size single-file maintenance risk is real.
PRICE_BENCHMARKS dict embedded inline in server.py — better suited to a config module or DB collection so non-engineers can update medians without code changes.

_check_rate_limit uses sync defaultdict in an async path — acceptable (no awaitable I/O) but in a multi-worker uvicorn deploy each worker has its own bucket → effective limit is N×5 not 5. Document or migrate to shared store.
Public decode endpoint coerces line items via try/except per row — good resilience pattern.
All new public endpoints honour the existing _detect_anomalies + Pydantic validation patterns — consistent with iter1 architecture.
Marketing palette change is scoped to CSS tokens (index.css + tailwind.config.js) — no component-level color hardcoding broke the existing product, confirmed visually.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 11 new public-tool tests — TestPublicBudget, TestPublicPriceCheck, TestPublicDecode, TestPublicRateLimit; uses x-forwarded-for IP isolation per test to avoid rate-limit collisions)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all marketing + tool + auth flows verified, zero JS errors

test_credentials: cathy@example.com / testpass123 (seeded). Public tools require no auth; rate-limit testing uses unique x-forwarded-for IPs (10.99.x.y).
seed_data_creation: Per-run test+<timestamp>@example.com signup + 'Mum' household. Public-tool tests use synthetic IPs and don't pollute Mongo (rate bucket is in-memory).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers 32 cases (auth/household/statements/budget/chat/family/audit/participant + public budget/price/decode/rate-limit). Public endpoint tests must pass an x-forwarded-for header to isolate IPs, otherwise the 5/30days bucket will block reruns. Login form testids are 'login-email-input' / 'login-password-input' / 'login-submit-button' (NOT 'login-email'). Sidebar nav testids: nav-dashboard, nav-statements, nav-ask-kindred, nav-family-thread, nav-audit-log. Marketing personas have testids persona-caregiver/persona-participant/persona-advisor; landing-hero decoder + countdown rely on text selectors (no testid yet).
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — iteration 2 is clean. New public layer + palette change integrated without regressing the authenticated product.
